title: Execute configured command on file events with token substitution
description: When a watched directory emits a create/modify/delete event, run the directory's configured command. Substitute {FILE} (basename), {PATH} (absolute path), {EVENT} (create|modify|delete), and {TIMESTAMP} (RFC 3339 UTC) before invocation.
design: Substitution is pure and unit-testable: split out a fn substitute(template: &str, ctx: &EventContext) -> String. Spawn with std::process::Command::new("sh").arg("-c").arg(rendered). Don't block the watcher thread waiting for the command to finish — spawn and let it run.
acceptance_criteria:
- Command runs via /bin/sh -c so shell features (pipes, redirection) work.
- All four substitution tokens replace correctly, even when paths contain spaces.
- Modify(Metadata) events are filtered out (per SPEC.md).
- Unit tests cover token substitution given a synthetic Event.
- Integration test: writing a file in a tempdir triggers the configured command and the command receives the right tokens.
- Command spawn failures are logged to stderr but do not crash the watcher.

title: Debounce bursty filesystem events before invoking the command
description: Obsidian (and most editors) save by writing to a temp file and renaming, producing 2-5 events per save. macOS FSEvents coalesces these; Linux inotify doesn't. Without debouncing, every save fires the command multiple times on Linux.
design: Use notify::debouncer_full (already a sibling crate in the notify ecosystem) instead of rolling our own. It wraps recommended_watcher and exposes a coalesced event stream. Decide debounce window via config (per-directory or global) with a sensible default.
acceptance_criteria:
- A configurable debounce window (default ~250ms) coalesces events per (path, event-kind) tuple.
- After the window with no new events for that key, fire one command invocation.
- Test verifies that N events within the window produce 1 invocation; N+1th event after window produces 2.
- Behavior verified on both Linux and macOS via CI.

title: Split rename events into delete-of-old + create-of-new
description: Per SPEC.md, renames should emit two command invocations: a 'delete' event for the old path and a 'create' event for the new one. v1 (bead f95) treats all renames as 'modify', because RenameMode::Both events carry [from, to] in event.paths and require per-path classification rather than a single event_kind for the whole Event.
design: Replace classify_event with a function that returns per-path classifications (e.g. Vec<(&Path, &'static str)>), or handle the rename case explicitly in handle_event before the general classification path. Either way, update SPEC.md to remove the v1 caveat once shipped.
acceptance_criteria:
- Modify(Name(RenameMode::From)) emits 'delete' for each path.
- Modify(Name(RenameMode::To)) emits 'create' for each path.
- Modify(Name(RenameMode::Both)) emits 'delete' for paths[0] and 'create' for paths[1].
- Existing 'classifies_rename_as_modify' test is replaced with per-RenameMode tests.
- Integration test verifies mv(1) of a file produces delete+create on Linux.
